days.title: Arrival in Barcelona
days.subtitle: Settling into the vibrant city by the sea

timeline.title: Check in to Hotel Europark
timeline.detail: Arrive and freshen up at this stylish hotel located just a 10-minute walk from the Passeig de Gràcia metro station.

timeline.title: Stroll at Parc de la Ciutadella
timeline.detail: Take a leisurely walk through this lush park, home to a stunning fountain designed by Gaudí. It's perfect for relaxing before dinner.
timeline.mapQuery: Parc de la Ciutadella, Barcelona, Spain

timeline.title: Beach time at Barceloneta
timeline.detail: Head to Barceloneta beach for some sun and surf. Enjoy a swim in the Mediterranean and soak up the atmosphere of beach bars lining the shore.

timeline.title: Dinner at Can Majó
timeline.detail: Savor traditional seafood dishes, including fresh paella, at this beachfront eatery. Don't miss trying their famous grilled prawns.

timeline.title: Evening walk along La Rambla
timeline.detail: Stroll along Barcelona's famous street filled with performers, shops, and cafes. It's alive with energy and a great way to end your first day.

localTips: Grab a T-10 transit card at the airport for discounted metro rides.
Book dinner at Can Majó in advance to secure a seaside table.
Check local events at Parc de la Ciutadella for free entertainment.

days.title: Exploring Gaudí's Masterpieces
days.subtitle: Art and architecture in Barcelona

timeline.title: Breakfast at Brunch & Cake
timeline.detail: Start your day with delicious brunch options at this popular café known for its colorful dishes and great coffee.

timeline.title: Visit Sagrada Familia
timeline.detail: Marvel at Gaudí's unfinished masterpiece. Spend time exploring the intricate details and try to book tickets in advance to skip the lines.

timeline.title: Lunch at El Nacional
timeline.detail: Enjoy a unique dining experience in this upscale space featuring several different restaurants in one. Sample various tapas from different culinary regions of Spain.

timeline.title: Explore Park Güell
timeline.detail: Discover the vibrant mosaics and whimsical designs of Gaudí’s park. Make sure to take plenty of photos and enjoy the panoramic views of the city.

timeline.title: Evening at Casa Batlló
timeline.detail: Visit this iconic building renowned for its unique architectural designs. Don’t forget to grab your tickets online to avoid long queues.

timeline.title: Dinner at Cervecería Catalana
timeline.detail: Experience Catalan cuisine with delicious tapas at this beloved local spot that offers a wide variety of small dishes bursting with flavor.

localTips: Purchase a combined ticket for Sagrada Familia and Park Güell for convenience.
Consider visiting Casa Batlló during the evening for beautiful lighting.
Check if El Nacional has any special events or cooking classes during your visit.

days.title: Culinary Delights and Departure
days.subtitle: Last taste of Barcelona

timeline.title: Breakfast at La Esquina
timeline.detail: Visit this cozy café for a peaceful breakfast before your last day of exploring. They serve great pastries and fresh coffee.

timeline.title: Food Tour in the Gothic Quarter
timeline.detail: Join a guided food tour to taste local specialties, including jamón ibérico and fresh fruits. It's a delicious dive into the region's culinary culture.

timeline.title: Lunch at La Paradeta
timeline.detail: Choose fresh seafood from the market and have it prepared on the spot at this casual yet popular diner offering a unique dining experience.

timeline.title: Final Shopping at El Corte Inglés
timeline.detail: Spend your last afternoon shopping at Spain’s famous department store where you can find everything from designer fashion to souvenirs.

timeline.title: Pack and Check out
timeline.detail: Return to your hotel to pack your bags and check out, ensuring you have all your belongings before heading to the airport.

timeline.title: Departure from Barcelona
timeline.detail: Heading to Barcelona Airport to catch your flight back home. Ensure you've allowed enough time for security checks.

localTips: Make sure to try churros with chocolate before you leave.
Keep an eye out for any local flea markets for unique finds.
Plan enough time to get to the airport considering Barcelona's traffic.

hotels.name: Hotel Europark
hotels.note: Stylish hotel within walking distance of the metro.
